name: clear-comments
description: Write code comments in anandyandawang's house style. Comment sparingly — the code says what, a comment says why. Vocabulary is `// TODO:` (with the unblocking condition, and the issue-tracker key in parens when the project uses one) and `// Note:` (an intentional, non-obvious decision); no FIXME/HACK/XXX, no `/* */` block comments, no commented-out code, no restating the line below. Backtick every code identifier. Doc comments are a mini-spec for public interfaces and complex services; inline `//` annotates mapping tables with the source system's label; tests get scenario/section banners. Always-on via hooks — keeps comments sparse and code self-documenting as a persistent default, not just when invoked.
